meta.title: Koshi Kogen Ski Area
meta.description: Skip Yuzawa and Naeba crowds—adult day ticket ¥2,000, 85% skiers, massive rentals. Yamakoshi, Nagaoka, Niigata.

hero.title:
Koshi Kogen
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Leave the Yuzawa queues behind—family-friendly snow in deep-snow Yamakoshi.

live.subtitle: Yamakoshi Tourism Development Corp. info (LP concept)

transit.title:
25 more minutes,
no lift lines.
transit.lead: ~25 minutes from Ojiya IC or Nagaoka-Minami-Koshiji SIC—thirty minutes beyond Yuzawa buys free parking, no queues, and wide slopes that feel private. Joetsu Shinkansen to Nagaoka ~1h40, then ~30 minutes by car (report ch. 6).
specs: ~30 min by car from Nagaoka Station
transit.imgAlt: Drive from Kan-etsu Expressway toward Yamakoshi—avoiding Yuzawa crowds

primary.title: 85% skiers—family psychological safety
primary.lead: 85% of visitors ski; 75% of reviews are families—a single-lift hill without park features screens for pure ski practice and beginner confidence. 55% beginner terrain, max 26° (report ch. 2).
primary.imgAlt: Wide mellow slope—families skiing at Koshi Kogen

secondary.title: Disruptive ¥2,000 and huge rentals
secondary.lead: Adult day ¥2,000, child ¥1,500—half mega-resort pricing. 244 skis, 209 boots (15–30 cm), ¥2,000 rentals each—¥6,000 all-in for adults. Free kids' lifts on the 3rd Sunday monthly (report ch. 3).

pass.title:
Adults ¥2,000,
children ¥1,500.
pass.lead: 12-ride books ¥2,000/¥1,500, single rides ¥200/¥150. Night tickets ¥1,500/¥1,000 (17:00–21:00). Season with night ¥18,000/¥13,000—a municipal loss-leader wrapping regional families (report ch. 3–4).
specs: Wed ladies' · Thu senior half price
pass.noticeBody: FY2024 ~¥3.5M deficit reframed as regional exchange-population cost—barbell strategy with Yomogi-hira Onsen lifts macro spend (report ch. 4–6).

rental.title:
244 skis, 209 boots,
¥2,000 each—travel light.
rental.lead: Ski set, board set, or outerwear ¥2,000 each—boots from 15 cm toddlers to 30 cm adults. Hats, goggles, and gloves BYO for hygiene and stock control.
rental.whisper: Lift plus full rental still ¥6,000 for a day—beats half-day mega-resort school fees (report ch. 3).

yukinko.title:
Wed/Fri/Sat nights,
17:00 to 21:00.
yukinko.lead: Night skiing Wed, Fri, Sat 17:00–21:00—the core winter activity keeping visitors in Yamakoshi after dark when terraces and alpaca farms are closed (report ch. 5).
yukinko.whisper: Closed Mon/Tue. Check koshikogen.com for latest hours.
yukinko.imgAlt: Lit night-ski slopes in Yamakoshi winter

family.title:
Kids' ski school,
¥1,000 per session (lift incl.).
family.lead: Kids' classes ages 5–elementary, 40 seats per session, badge-test courses ¥2,000—early success builds lifelong place attachment. Onigiri hut weekends and kids' hill with local food.
family.noticeBody: Vs ¥5,000–7,000 half-day schools at mega-resorts—commune pricing beats travel cost delta (report ch. 3).

workation.title:
Skip Yuzawa queues,
add 30 calm minutes.
workation.lead: Exit Kan-etsu at Ojiya or drive from Nagaoka—free parking, no lift lines, slopes that feel yours alone vs an hour in Yuzawa queues (report ch. 6).

local.title: Yamakoshi DAO × ski hill guide
local.body: Nishikigoi NFT and 1,000+ digital villagers—winter trial stays center on Koshi Kogen snow play. Near-zero CAC community-driven visits (report ch. 4).

schools.title: Yomogi-hira Onsen barbell
schools.body: Low-cost skiing × high-end ryokan (Izumiya, Yomoyama-kan, Fukubikiya)—maximize regional spend beyond lift revenue alone (report ch. 5–6).

summer.title: Winter ski to summer Yamakoshi
summer.body: Terraces and alpaca farms sleep in winter—the ski hill is the main active attraction. Pair with Orataru recovery museum for edu-tourism (report ch. 5).

access.note: ~25 min from Ojiya IC / Nagaoka-Minami-Koshiji SIC · ~30 min from JR Nagaoka
